title: Victim/Offender Mediation Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Legal & Public Safety > Victim/Offender Mediation Programs 4 Matching Service Providers Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CITY OF BEAVERTON CENTER FOR MEDIATION AND DIALOGUE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential mediation, facilitation, and conflict resolution education. Types of mediation include: Community Mediation: Conflicts involving noise, fences, parking, vandalism, animal complaints, property damage or maintenance, harassment, minor assaults, and conflicts over money and personal property. Foreclosure Avoidance Mediation: Utilizes specially trained facilitators to conduct face-to-face resolution conferences between borrowers and lenders to avoid home foreclosure. Housing Mediation: For tenants, landlords/housing providers wishing to discuss issues related to rent, repairs, communication, or any other issues or concerns related to the living arrangement. Manufactured Home (MFH) Mediation: Assists homeowners, residents, managers, and park owners to resolve conflicts associated with the parks and living situations. Home Sharing Mediation: Connects homeless high school students who are committed to graduating from high school with people willing to share their homes. Mediators facilitate rental contracts and a house rules discussion to provide a space for open communication and mutual expectation setting before move-in. Mediators check in with the parties after a few months to continue promoting open communication, contract modifications, and conflict resolution strategies as needed. Restorative Dialogue: Offers trained facilitators to help juvenile offenders and crime victims meet face-to-face. The dialogue is an opportunity to discuss the impacts of the harm, meaningful accountability, and make agreements to repair the harm. Takes most cases, with the exceptions of parenting plan, divorce cases, or court-ordered small claims, which are handled through Washington County. 12725 SW Millikan Way Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5:30pm Email (503) 526-2523 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY MEDIATION SERVICES FOR POLK COUNTY COMMUNITY MEDIATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Trained mediators assist in finding solutions to conflicts. Mediators have experience with interpersonal disputes, family mediation (e.g., parent/teen, runaways), parenting plan adjustments, neighborhood disputes (e.g., barking dogs, property line/easement disputes), landlord/tenant disputes, institutional/business mediation, and victim/offender mediation. Also holds workshops, public lectures, and discussions on mediation and coaches individuals in conflicted situations. Meetings may be held in the client's community to ease time or transportation limitations. Does not mediate domestic violence cases. 320 SE Fir Villa Road Dallas, OR 97338 Eligibility: Must be a resident of or employed in Polk County Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm. Special arrangements outside these hours may be possible. Email (503) 623-3111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON MEDIATION CENTER MEDIATION AND RESTORATIVE JUSTICE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Dispute resolution center offering mediation services through trained volunteer mediators. Mediates small claims, neighbor-to-neighbor disputes, harassment claims, vandalism, property conflicts, family relationships, animal disputes, employee disputes, landlord/tenant disputes, student disputes, noise issues, debt, youth disputes (including parent/teen conflict), and parking disputes. Also offers restorative justice conferencing for victims, allowing them to confront their offenders in a restorative setting. 1007 4th Street La Grande, OR 97850 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Phone hours: Monday-Friday 7am-8pm Email (541) 786-0270 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINCOLN COMMUNITY DISPUTE RESOLUTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Dispute resolution and agreement development in the following areas: Housing, Workplace, Neighbors, Family Members, Youth, Businesses, and Government. Also offers Conflict Resolution training and group meeting facilitation with emphasis on consensus-building. 111 SE Douglas Street Newport, OR 97365 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: By appointment only. Services are scheduled to accommodate needs of client Email (541) 574-9846 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF BEAVERTON CENTER FOR MEDIATION AND DIALOGUE COMMUNITY MEDIATION SERVICES FOR POLK COUNTY COMMUNITY MEDIATION SERVICES EASTERN OREGON MEDIATION CENTER MEDIATION AND RESTORATIVE JUSTICE SERVICES LINCOLN COMMUNITY DISPUTE RESOLUTION LINCOLN COMMUNITY DISPUTE RESOLUTION
